assertions.evidence: Explicitly walks through 'Phase 1 — Record' through 'Phase 8 — Publish' with a summary table listing all 8 phases.
The document does not mention HARNESS.md by name. It follows the methodology implicitly but never references the document.

Identifies Google session cookies (SID, HSID, SSID, APISID, SAPISID), Authorization: Bearer headers, CSRF tokens, and SAPISIDHASH computation. States 'Google auth is the central challenge for this CLI.'

CLI command structure section shows 'notebooks list/get/create/rename/delete', 'sources list/add-url/add-pdf/add-text/get/delete', 'ask --notebook --query', and 'audio generate/status/download' command groups.

assertions.text: Mentions Chrome DevTools MCP for traffic capture
assertions.evidence: Phase 1 states: 'Call navigate (chrome-devtools MCP)' and 'begin network monitoring via read_network_requests'. Also mentions using 'javascript_tool' to extract cookies.

Header states 'Python namespace: cli_web.notebooklm'. Package structure shows 'cli_web/notebooklm/' with all modules. Notes 'cli_web/ has NO __init__.py — this is required for the namespace package pattern.'
Uses its own 5-phase implementation plan (Weeks 1-5: Foundation, Sources, Q&A, Audio, Polish) and a 12-section document structure. Does not mention the 8-phase pipeline.
No mention of HARNESS.md anywhere in the document.

Section 1.2 states 'There is no OAuth2 client_id for NotebookLM. The only viable auth path is... CLI extracts session cookies... CLI will be session-cookie authenticated, not token-authenticated.' Mentions __Secure-1PSID, __Secure-3PSID cookies.

Sections 5.1-5.4 define 'notebooklm notebook list/create/show/rename/delete', 'notebooklm source add/list/show/delete', 'notebooklm ask' and 'notebooklm chat', and 'notebooklm audio generate/status/download/delete'.

assertions.text: Mentions Chrome DevTools MCP for traffic capture
assertions.evidence: Section 4.1 mentions 'Open Chrome DevTools → Network tab' as a manual methodology, but does not mention Chrome DevTools MCP (the tool/protocol). Uses Playwright instead for browser automation.
Uses 'notebooklm_cli/' as the package name (e.g., 'notebooklm_cli/cli.py'). Does not use or mention the cli_web.notebooklm namespace pattern.

assertions.text: Produces TEST.md Part 1 structure (inventory, unit plan, E2E plan, scenarios)
assertions.evidence: Document titled 'TEST.md — cli-web-notebooklm, Part 1: Test Plan (Phase 5)'. Contains sections: 1. Test Inventory, 2. Unit Test Plan, 3. E2E Test Plan, 4. Realistic Workflow Scenarios, 5. Coverage Targets, 6. Test Infrastructure Notes.
Section 3.3 includes the full _resolve_cli helper code with CLI_WEB_FORCE_INSTALLED support, shutil.which lookup, and fallback to python -m. TestCLISubprocess class shown with _run helper.

assertions.text: Includes realistic workflow scenario (create notebook → add source → query)
assertions.evidence: Scenario A: 'New Notebook Lifecycle' includes create notebook → add URL source → list sources → delete. Scenario D: 'JSON Output for Agent Consumption' includes create → add source → list sources → delete. (No explicit query/ask step in scenarios, but create→add source→verify is covered.)

Section 1 lists fixture files to create in 'tests/fixtures/'. Section 3.1 describes 'Fixture Replay Tests' that 'load pre-captured API responses from tests/fixtures/ and replay them through the real parsing/command logic. No network required.'

assertions.text: States tests must FAIL (not skip) without auth
assertions.evidence: Section 3.2: 'Auth is required — tests FAIL (do not skip) if ~/.config/cli-web-notebooklm/auth.json is absent.' Section 6: 'Live tests load credentials... If the file is absent, tests fail — they do not skip. This is by design (HARNESS.md Rules).'

Section 2.3: 'test_create_notebook_returns_entity_with_submitted_name — Returned entity name matches what was submitted (response body verification)'. Scenario A verified section: 'Submitted name propagates to read-back (not server-generated default)'. Multiple tests verify specific response fields, not just status codes.

assertions.text: Produces TEST.md Part 1 structure (inventory, unit plan, E2E plan, scenarios)
assertions.evidence: Document titled 'TEST.md — cli-web-notebooklm (Part 1: Test Plan)'. Contains: 1. Test Inventory, 2. Unit Test Plan, 3. E2E Test Plan, 4. Realistic Workflow Scenarios, 5. Edge Cases Inventory, 6. Test Infrastructure Requirements.
Section 3.3 includes the full _resolve_cli helper code with CLI_WEB_FORCE_INSTALLED, shutil.which, RuntimeError fallback, and TestCLISubprocess class with _run helper.

assertions.text: Includes realistic workflow scenario (create notebook → add source → query)
assertions.evidence: Scenario 1 covers 'Full Notebook Lifecycle' (create → read → list → delete → 404). Scenario 2 covers 'Add URL Source'. Scenario 4 covers 'Multiple Sources'. However, none of the scenarios include a query/chat/ask step — they only cover notebook CRUD and source management.
Section 3.1: 'These tests load JSON from tests/fixtures/ and replay them through the full command stack — no real network.' Lists 10 required fixture files under 'tests/fixtures/'.

assertions.text: States tests must FAIL (not skip) without auth
assertions.evidence: Section 3.2: 'They FAIL (not skip) if ~/.config/cli-web-notebooklm/auth.json is absent or invalid.' Section 7 rejection criteria: 'pytest.skip() on missing auth — tests MUST pytest.fail() with instructions'.

Section 7 rejection criteria: 'Fixture tests that do not verify specific response body fields (checking status 200 alone is not sufficient)'. Section 3.2 states: 'every live test MUST do create → read back → verify fields. Tests that only create without reading back are not accepted.'
Category 2 Required Files table: '__main__.py | FAIL | Missing'. Listed as item #2 in the 9 missing files list.

Category 2: 'core/session.py | FAIL | Missing' and 'core/models.py | FAIL | Missing'. Category 4: 'session.py: Session class with undo/redo stack | FAIL | File does not exist' and 'models.py: typed response models | FAIL | File does not exist'.

assertions.text: Identifies missing utils/ directory with repl_skin.py, output.py, config.py
assertions.evidence: Category 1: 'commands/ and utils/ directories are missing entirely'. Category 2 lists: 'utils/repl_skin.py | FAIL | Missing (entire utils/ dir missing)', 'utils/output.py | FAIL | Missing', 'utils/config.py | FAIL | Missing'.
Category 1: 'commands/ and utils/ directories are missing entirely'. Summary table item #5: 'cli_web/notebooklm/commands/ (directory + at least one module) | Directory Structure'.
Category 2: 'tests/TEST.md | FAIL | Missing' and 'tests/test_e2e.py | FAIL | Missing'. Category 5 confirms both are missing with detailed notes.

assertions.text: Identifies missing README.md inside the package
assertions.evidence: Category 2: 'README.md | FAIL | Missing'. Category 6: 'README.md: installation, auth setup, command reference, examples | FAIL | Missing entirely'. Summary item #1: 'cli_web/notebooklm/README.md'.
Opening line: 'Checked against the 50-check quality checklist (8 categories) from the cli-anything-web-standards skill.' Summary shows 'Confirmed failing checks: 22 of 50'.

assertions.text: Gives actionable suggestions (not just lists missing files)
assertions.evidence: Category 3: 'Copy it from ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/repl_skin.py'. Category 4: 'must implement a Session class with undo/redo stack'. Category 7: 'Confirm find_namespace_packages is used'. Category 8: specific bash commands to run for verification. Final note: 'You must resolve all 22 confirmed failures before the package will pass /cli-anything-web:validate'.
Category 2 table: 'cli_web/notebooklm/__main__.py | MISSING'. Item #2 in the 9 missing files list.

Category 2: 'cli_web/notebooklm/core/session.py | MISSING' and 'cli_web/notebooklm/core/models.py | MISSING'. Category 4: 'session.py: Session class with undo/redo stack | FAIL — file does not exist' and 'models.py: typed response models | FAIL — file does not exist'.

assertions.text: Identifies missing utils/ directory with repl_skin.py, output.py, config.py
assertions.evidence: Category 1: 'commands/ and utils/ are missing'. Category 2: 'cli_web/notebooklm/utils/repl_skin.py | MISSING', 'utils/output.py | MISSING', 'utils/config.py | MISSING'. Complete missing files tree shows entire utils/ directory.
Category 1: 'commands/ and utils/ are missing — Click command group files, one per API resource'. Complete missing files tree: 'commands/ MISSING (entire directory)'.
Category 2: 'cli_web/notebooklm/tests/TEST.md | MISSING' and 'tests/test_e2e.py | MISSING'. Category 5 dedicates extensive detail to both.

assertions.text: Identifies missing README.md inside the package
assertions.evidence: Category 2: 'cli_web/notebooklm/README.md | MISSING'. Category 6: 'README.md: installation, auth setup, command reference, examples | FAIL — file missing'. Complete missing files tree confirms it.
Opening: 'Evaluated against the 8-category, 50-check standard defined in HARNESS.md'. Summary table shows all 8 categories with passing/total counts out of 50.

assertions.text: Gives actionable suggestions (not just lists missing files)
assertions.evidence: Category 4: detailed specs for what session.py and models.py must contain. Category 5: 'Response verification requirement — Never trust HTTP 200 alone' with create/read/update/delete verification rules. Category 7: full setup.py example code. Category 8: specific bash commands to run. Standards list enumerates 18 specific standards to implement.
